title: Chionanthus globosus
intro: Chionanthus globosus, also known as the Chinese parasol tree, is a medicinal plant species native to China, belonging to the family Oleaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues, fever, and other conditions. Bioactive constituents of Chionanthus globosus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, such as chionanthine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and capsules, often in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Chionanthus globosus is a member of the family Oleaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the olive family. This family consists of around 660 species, including many popular ornamental plants.
